heading: Removal of officer from list of selectees for promotion
label: 2122 Removal of officer from list of selectees for promotion
notes: Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 112(b)section 272 of this titleSubsecs. (a), (b). , substituted “section 2121” for “section 271”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2)

Delegation of AuthorityAuthority of President under subsec. (a) of this section to remove a name of an officer from a list of selectees delegated to Secretary of Homeland Security by Ex. Ord. No. 14106, § 1(b), , 88 F.R. 55905, set out in a note under .Aug. 14, 2023section 2101 of this title
amendments: Amendments2018—, renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282, § 112(b)section 272 of this titleSubsecs. (a), (b). , substituted “section 2121” for “section 271”.Pub. L. 115–282, § 123(b)(2)

children.label: a
children.text: The President may remove the name of any officer from a list of selectees established under .section 2121 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: If the Senate does not consent to the appointment of an officer whose name is on a list of selectees established under , that officer’s name shall be removed from this list.section 2121 of this title

children.label: c
children.text: An officer whose name is removed from a list under subsection (a) or (b) continues to be eligible for consideration for promotion. If he is selected for promotion by the next selection board and promoted, he shall be given the date of rank and position on the active duty promotion list in the grade to which promoted that he would have held if his name had not been removed. However, if the officer is not selected by the next selection board or if his name is again removed from the list of selectees, he shall be considered for all purposes as having twice failed of selection for promotion.
